text_block: The venom of a Box Jellyfish is so potent it can cause <red>cardiac arrest</red> and death within minutes, long before you can reach the <yellow>shoreline.</yellow>
The bobbit worm buries its ten-foot-long body in the seafloor, waiting to launch an explosive strike that can <red>slice prey clean in half</red> with its <yellow>scissor-like jaws</yellow>.
Drinking your own urine in a survival crisis is a myth; its high salt concentration will actually <red>overload your kidneys</red> and severely accelerate <yellow>deadly dehydration</yellow>.

The gulper eel thrives in the pitch-black midnight zone, utilizing a jaw that stretches to swallow prey <yellow>larger than its own body</yellow> whole, slowly digesting them in an <red>expandable stomach</red>.
Rubbing snow on frostbitten skin is a dangerous myth; the sharp ice crystals will tear your damaged tissue, causing <red>permanent nerve damage</red> and worsening <yellow>tissue necrosis</yellow>.

The emerald cockroach wasp delivers a precise sting directly into a roach's brain, stripping its free will so the wasp can lead it like a dog to a <red>living burial</red> where its larva <yellow>eats it from the inside</yellow>.

yt_search_query: horned lizard squirts blood from eyes
text_block: To deter predators, the horned lizard can deliberately rupture tiny blood vessels in its eyes to <red>squirt foul-tasting blood</red> up to five feet away, targeting the predator's <yellow>sensitive mouth and eyes</yellow>.

If caught in a powerful rip current, swimming directly back to shore is fatal; you must swim <yellow>parallel to the beach</yellow> to escape its pull before you exhaust your energy and <red>drown</red>.

When attacked, the ancient hagfish secretes a micro-fibrous slime that expands ten thousand times in seconds, instantly <red>clogging the gills</red> of predators and forcing them to <yellow>choke to death</yellow>.

yt_search_query: hooded pitohui poisonous bird feather toxin
text_block: The hooded pitohui of New Guinea is one of the world's only poisonous birds, harboring the same <red>deadly neurotoxins</red> as poison dart frogs within its feathers and skin to cause <yellow>numbness and paralysis</yellow> upon touch.

yt_search_query: hair standing on end lightning strike warning
text_block: If your hair suddenly stands on end in an open field, a lightning strike is imminent; you must immediately crouch low on the balls of your feet to minimize <red>ground current contact</red> and avoid a <yellow>fatal cardiac event</yellow>.
